review_questions: Does CP00-142 map all 150 planned UI fixture/evidence and P05 fixture units in plan order?
Can any CP00-142 row load fixture payloads, materialize golden-case payloads, run replay commands, execute AI retrieval, execute analytics queries, or write audit/product state?
Are tenant, user, matter, document, golden, denied, cross-tenant, audit hint, security trimming, AI retrieval, and analytics cases descriptor-only?
Does CP00-142 preserve hidden source fields and unauthorized count/object name suppression?
Does the pack hand off cleanly from CP00-141 to CP00-143 without implementing LDIP or splitting HRX?

findings.title: Hermes evidence packet references a cp00-142 manifest absent from the staged diff
findings.description: createAuditComplianceCp142HermesEvidencePacket() lists "docs/closeout-packs/cp00-142/manifest.json" in evidence_refs, but that manifest is neither in the staged diff nor in git status. Both the test (packages/audit/test/audit.test.js) and validate-rp03-audit-architecture.mjs read this path inside try/catch and fall back to closeout-pack-plan.json (plan_binding_snapshot), so coverage validation succeeds via the plan entry. The reference is a descriptor string, not a live file read, so there is no runtime failure. Non-blocking: this only matters if pack convention requires shipping a per-pack manifest.json artifact alongside the catalog.
findings.recommendation: Confirm the cp00-142 manifest is produced by the closeout step (consistent with cp00-135..141), or accept the documented plan-fallback path as the source of truth for coverage binding.

findings.title: behavior_kind "replay_command" has no STATUS_BY_KIND entry and uses the generic fallback status
findings.description: FIXTURE_WORKFLOW_UNITS index 22 ("Replay command") slugs to behavior_kind "replay_command", which is not a key in STATUS_BY_KIND. expected_status therefore falls back to "replay_command_ui_fixture_evidence_reference_bound". This is cosmetic only: the fallback still ends with "_bound", so all_cases_ready stays true and no assertion breaks. Naming is slightly inconsistent with the P05 fixture-domain rows (which otherwise use *_fixture_reference_bound), but carries no functional impact.
findings.recommendation: Optional: add an explicit STATUS_BY_KIND entry (e.g. replay_command: "replay_command_fixture_reference_bound") for naming consistency with the other P05 fixture rows.

parsed_review.adjudication_notes:
PASS / approve. No P0, P1, or P2 findings. Every checklist item verified against the staged diff:

1. UNIT COVERAGE (150): Hand-verified the 11 micro-phase slices sum to exactly 150 — M06(5)+M07(22)+M08(20)+M09(20)+M10(8)+P05.M00(4)+P05.M01(8)+P05.M02(8)+P05.M03(22)+P05.M04(20)+P05.M05(13). First unit RP03.P04.M06.S16, last RP03.P05.M05.S13, all IDs unique. deliverable_counts (fixture40/test10/hermes6/claude13/impl38/ui31/sec12) and evidence_mode_counts both re-derived from the templates and confirmed to sum to 150 and match the test assertions. validateAuditComplianceCp142Coverage enforces count, endpoints, uniqueness, and per-index plan-order equality; validation reportedly passed, so generated IDs match the plan enumeration.

2. NO-WRITE FIXTURE/EVIDENCE DESCRIPTORS: Code is purely declarative (frozen objects, maps, counts) — no fs writes, no network, no DB, no execution. The no-write attestation (47 flags) is asserted in three independent places (contract block, CP142_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION + freezeNoWriteResult, and the validation script field loop) and all are false, including the C-risk-specific loads_fixture_payload / materializes_golden_case_payload / executes_replay_command / executes_ai_retrieval / executes_analytics_query. Per-case results add unauthorized_count_exposed / unauthorized_object_name_exposed / hidden_field_names_exposed = false.

3. HIDDEN FIELD LEAKAGE: CP142_HIDDEN_SOURCE_FIELDS is a denylist of 26 field NAMES (incl. raw_document_body, secret, credential, payment_card_number, bank_account_number, access_token, private_key, cross_tenant_row_count, unauthorized_object_name). These are policy identifiers, not values; no actual sensitive value or count is ever materialized. runCase results omit the denylist and set hidden_field_names_exposed:false. The contract mirrors the list and the validator requires its presence. No leakage.

4. H03/C03 GATE BINDING: hermes_gate "H03" / claude_gate "C03" consistent across PACK_BINDING, the contract, the Hermes packet (evidence_id H03.CP00-142...), the Claude packet (review_id C03.CP00-142..., model claude-opus-4-8, effort max, read_only true, executes_review/sends_claude_prompt false), readiness (h03/c03_gate_bound true), and the validation script.

5. CP00-143 HANDOFF: next_pack_id CP00-143, next_subphase_id RP03.P05.M05.S14 — exactly one unit past the covered range end (S13). Chain continuity confirmed: prior pack CP00-141 declared next_subphase RP03.P04.M06.S16, which is CP00-142's first unit; upstream_pack_id CP00-141 / cp141_handoff_inherited true. Contract current_pack, schema bump v0.8→v0.9, and validator expectations all updated consistently.

6. HRX NOT SPLIT / LDIP NOT IMPLEMENTED: splits_hrx_product:false and implements_ldip:false in the contract attestation, the module attestation, freezeNoWriteResult, and the validation field loop.

The two P3 items above are informational and do not block closeout. Note: the staged diff does not modify closeout-pack-plan.json, so the CP00-142 plan entry (150 units, risk C) must already exist upstream for the asserted validation to pass; this is consistent with the pack's plan-fallback design and the stated pre-review validation run.

Verdict: **valid_review = true**, no P0/P1/P2 findings — CP00-142 is a clean, internally consistent Risk-C no-write reference pack. The only two notes are P3 informational (graceful manifest fallback; one cosmetic status-naming fallback), neither blocking.
